data.narrative:
Dawn Aerospace, a Series B space & aerospace company that has raised $30M+, is valued here through comparison to a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector.

Two valuation methods triangulate on a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $30M total raised proxy, yielding $57M. Peer-median anchoring uses three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—to establish a $400M median, with an observed range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; the Space & Aerospace public set trades at a 49.8x price-to-sales median across 6 tickers, but Dawn's revenue is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $48M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $228M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty in early-stage space venture valuation. The peer-median method dominates the upper bound; stage-progression conservatively anchors the floor. Key uncertainties include Dawn's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to the noted peer set, particularly versus higher-valued outliers like Aalyria. A midpoint estimate of approximately $230M reflects equal weighting of applicable methods absent additional operational data.
